results.query: My fridge broke down three weeks after I bought it — should I use VoltCare or just return it?
results.rewritten_query: fridge hardware failure warranty coverage VoltCare versus Consumer Rights Act return refund three weeks after purchase
results.suggested_response: Since your fridge broke down within 30 days of purchase, you have the right to a full refund under the Consumer Rights Act. You can choose a refund, repair, or replacement - it's your choice. If you prefer, VoltCare is also available, but your statutory right to reject gives you stronger protection at this stage.
results.notes: Cross-category: within 30 days so statutory rights stronger than VoltCare — agent should present both

results.query: I dropped my VoltMobile phone and smashed the screen — is that covered anywhere?
results.rewritten_query: VoltMobile phone accidental damage screen breakage coverage VoltCare protection plan
results.suggested_response: If you have VoltCare Plus or Premium on your phone, accidental damage like a smashed screen is covered. There would be an excess charge based on your phone's original price. If you don't have VoltCare or only have Essential, accidental damage isn't covered. Would you like me to check what protection you have?
results.notes: Cross-category: VoltCare AD claim or VoltMobile device insurance — both options should be mentioned

results.query: My new washing machine arrived but it's making a banging noise — do I return it or get it repaired?
results.rewritten_query: washing machine excessive noise banging sound manufacturing defect return repair warranty statutory rights
results.suggested_response: A banging noise on a new washing machine sounds like a manufacturing fault. Since it's new, you have the right to a full refund, or repair/replacement if you prefer. We can arrange collection and disconnection at no charge for faulty returns within 30 days. Would you like to proceed with a return?
results.notes: Cross-category: fault on new delivery — statutory rights apply, present return and repair options

results.query: I spilled coffee on my laptop keyboard — some keys have stopped working
results.rewritten_query: laptop keyboard liquid damage coffee spill key failure hardware damage warranty coverage repair claim
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry to hear about your laptop. Coffee spills are classed as accidental damage. If you have VoltCare Plus or Complete, this would be covered with one claim per year (excess applies to Plus). Please don't turn the device on after liquid damage. I can help you start a claim if you have the right cover.
results.notes: Liquid damage = accidental damage — covered under Plus/Complete, not Essential

results.query: Can I get VoltCare on a laptop I bought second hand from someone else?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare eligibility second hand laptop third party purchase proof of purchase original warranty
results.suggested_response: Unfortunately, VoltCare is only available on laptops purchased new from Voltex stores or voltex.co.uk. Laptops not purchased from Voltex aren't eligible for any VoltCare plan. However, you still have statutory rights under the Consumer Rights Act that may protect you.
results.notes: Trap: second-hand not from Voltex = not eligible. Transfer only works if originally bought from Voltex.

results.query: My VoltCare payment failed last week and now my tumble dryer has broken — can I still claim?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare payment failure missed payment tumble dryer breakdown claim eligibility cover lapsed
results.suggested_response: Good news - you can still claim! VoltCare has a Grace Period (days 11-25 after missed payment) where claims proceed normally. A payment problem doesn't suspend your right to claim during this time. Let me start your claim process right away.
results.notes: Grace Period — cover continues for 14 days after payment failure. Critical policy detail.

results.query: I got a TV for Christmas and I don't like it — can I swap it for something different?
results.rewritten_query: Christmas gift purchase return exchange different product Consumer Rights Act
results.suggested_response: Since your TV was a Christmas gift, you can return it until 31st January under our extended Christmas returns policy. You'll need the receipt or order confirmation. The refund goes back to the original payment method unless a gift receipt was provided, which would give you store credit for an exchange.
results.notes: Christmas extended returns window — up to 31 January. Exchange processed as return + new purchase.

results.query: I paid for my laptop using buy now pay later — how does the refund work if I return it?
results.rewritten_query: buy now pay later BNPL refund return process payment method
results.suggested_response: When you return your laptop, your VoltPay finance agreement will be adjusted or cancelled depending on whether you used finance for the full amount or part payment. Duologi will handle this automatically and send you updated details within 5 business days if needed.
results.notes: VoltPay return process — finance agreement cancelled, Duologi refunds payments

results.query: I had my phone screen fixed at a local repair shop and now it's broken again — Voltex said it voids my warranty
results.rewritten_query: unauthorised repair third party voided warranty coverage phone screen repair local shop
results.suggested_response: I understand your concern about the warranty. Let me check your specific policy details and coverage options. I'll need to review your account and the circumstances to give you accurate information about what's covered.
results.escalation_reason: Knowledge base lacks specific information about unauthorised repair impact on warranty coverage - requires specialist review of policy terms
results.notes: Trap: agent cannot void cover on call — only repair team can confirm after inspection. Must escalate.

results.query: Is 8GB RAM enough for a new laptop in 2026?
results.rewritten_query: 8GB RAM laptop specification memory requirements 2026 performance adequacy
results.suggested_response: For a new laptop in 2026, I wouldn't recommend 8GB RAM. While it meets Windows 11 minimum requirements, it can feel sluggish with everyday multitasking like multiple browser tabs. 16GB is our recommended standard - it handles daily tasks much more comfortably and gives better future-proofing.
results.notes: Clear FAQ answer — 8GB not recommended for new purchases in 2026

results.query: My mum wants a washing machine that doesn't need defrosting — what should she look for?
results.rewritten_query: washing machine frost free automatic defrost feature product specifications
results.suggested_response: I think there might be some confusion here - washing machines don't need defrosting as they don't have freezer compartments. You might be thinking of fridge-freezers? If your mum needs a fridge-freezer, I'd recommend looking for 'frost-free' or 'Total No Frost' models that prevent ice build-up automatically.
results.notes: Colloquial phrasing for frost-free feature — query rewriter must translate correctly

results.query: I want to watch football on my new TV — what specs should I look for?
results.rewritten_query: TV specifications football sports viewing refresh rate motion handling picture quality
results.suggested_response: For watching football, you'll want a TV with native 120Hz refresh rate for smooth motion during fast action. Look for high brightness displays like QLED or Mini-LED that work well in bright rooms. Also consider low input lag if you use external speakers. What size TV and budget are you thinking?
results.notes: Sport viewing requirements — native 120Hz is the key spec, not motion processing

results.query: Will a Chromebook work for my daughter who needs to use Microsoft Office for school?
results.rewritten_query: Chromebook Microsoft Office compatibility school software requirements student laptop
results.suggested_response: Chromebooks can access Microsoft Office through the web app version, but this has reduced functionality compared to the full desktop application. If your daughter needs the complete Office suite features for school assignments, a Windows laptop would be better suited to ensure full compatibility.
results.notes: Nuanced: Office web app works on Chromebook but not full desktop app — important distinction

results.query: I ordered a phone online yesterday and changed my mind — how do I cancel?
results.rewritten_query: order cancellation online purchase change of mind refund return policy
results.suggested_response: No problem! Since you ordered online, you have 14 days from delivery to cancel for any reason under your statutory rights, plus we offer a 30-day goodwill return window. If your phone hasn't been delivered yet, I can cancel the order now. If it's already shipped, you can return it once received. Would you like me to cancel it now?
results.notes: Simple cancellation before despatch

results.query: The delivery driver left my laptop on my doorstep without knocking — it got rained on
results.rewritten_query: delivery driver failed safe delivery protocol unattended doorstep weather damage
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry this happened - our delivery team should never leave large items unattended. Since this was our delivery team's error and your laptop was damaged as a result, we'll arrange a replacement for you. Let me get this sorted right away.
results.escalation_reason: Delivery protocol breach resulting in product damage requires Team Leader authorisation for replacement
results.notes: Safe place delivery resulting in damage — carrier liability issue, needs escalation

results.query: I need a washing machine urgently — my old one flooded and I have three kids
results.rewritten_query: washing machine urgent purchase availability stock delivery same day next day three children household
results.suggested_response: I understand this is urgent with three children. For a family of your size, I'd recommend an 8-10kg washing machine. Let me check our current stock and fastest delivery options for you right now. Can you confirm your postcode for delivery availability?
results.notes: Urgent delivery request — agent should check for earliest available slot and mention VoltCare Complete priority

results.query: I am going to Dubai for two weeks — will my VoltMobile work there?
results.rewritten_query: Zone A roaming VoltMobile phone usage abroad UAE Dubai
results.suggested_response: Yes, your VoltMobile will work in Dubai! The UAE is in Zone A, so you can get a Roaming Pass A for £12 covering your full 14-day trip. This includes 5GB data and unlimited calls and texts. You can buy it through the VoltMobile app or by calling 0333 400 9000 before you travel.
results.notes: Dubai = UAE = Zone A roaming pass £12 for 14 days — not EU roaming

results.query: My VoltMobile bill is way higher than usual this month — I don't understand why
results.rewritten_query: VoltMobile bill increase unexpected charges monthly billing dispute
results.suggested_response: I'll help you understand your bill increase. Let me check your account to see what charges have been applied this month. Your bill includes your base plan, any add-ons like data boosts or roaming passes, and any third-party services. Can you confirm which specific charges look unusual to you?
results.notes: Unexpected bill — must pull usage records before confirming or denying any charge

results.query: I want to move to a different network and keep my number — how long does that take and what happens to my VoltMobile account?
results.rewritten_query: number porting PAC code network transfer VoltMobile account cancellation timeline
results.suggested_response: You can keep your number when switching networks. First, request a PAC code by texting PAC to 65075 - it's free and you'll get it within 2 hours. Give this PAC to your new network to transfer your number. Your VoltMobile account will close once the port completes, but you have 30 days to use the PAC even after closure.
results.notes: Porting away — PAC within 2 hours, port completes next working day, number preserved 30 days after closure

results.query: I have been with VoltMobile for 3 years and I'm thinking of leaving — can you do anything to keep me?
results.rewritten_query: customer retention goodwill discretionary VoltMobile contract cancellation
results.suggested_response: I really appreciate your 3 years of loyalty with VoltMobile! I'd love to help explore your options to keep you with us. Let me check what we might be able to do - could you tell me what's prompting you to consider leaving so I can look into the best solutions for you?
results.escalation_reason: Customer retention requests typically require Team Leader authorisation for discretionary offers or account adjustments not covered in standard policy documentation
results.notes: Retention scenario — must provide PAC if requested, retention offer permitted but not conditional on withholding PAC
